title: Content Recency / Up-to-Date (no outdated info)
guideline_note: Rule 20, added by the user on 2026-07-13: cross-check that the content reflects the LATEST standards/versions and does not present old, deprecated, or superseded information as current.
grader_note: Best when the live web market check is enabled (harness model.enable_web_market_check=true); otherwise the judge relies on the model's trained knowledge, which has a cutoff — so 'latest' is verified as-of that cutoff. Flag anything clearly deprecated or superseded.
description: Facts, versions, standards, tools, and recommendations must reflect the CURRENT state of the topic. Deprecated/obsolete material must not be presented as current; if legacy is mentioned, it must be labelled as legacy/historical, not as the present standard.
criterion: No superseded standard/version presented as current; current versions named where relevant; deprecated items explicitly marked as deprecated/legacy.

rubric.5: Reflects current standards/versions; any legacy item is clearly labelled as legacy.
rubric.3: Mostly current, but one dated detail not flagged as legacy.
rubric.1: Presents a deprecated/superseded standard or version as the current one.

input.text: TLS 1.3 (RFC 8446) is the current version; TLS 1.0/1.1 are deprecated (RFC 8996), and SSL is obsolete.

cases.rationale: Names the current standard and correctly labels the old ones as deprecated/obsolete.

input.text: HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 (over QUIC) are current; HTTP/1.1 is still widely used but older.

cases.rationale: Current versions named; legacy correctly framed.

input.text: SSL 3.0 is the standard protocol for securing web traffic.

cases.rationale: SSL is deprecated/insecure and superseded by TLS — presenting it as the standard is outdated.

input.text: HTTP/1.1 is the latest version of the HTTP protocol.

cases.rationale: Ignores HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 — stale claim presented as current.

input.text: Use Python 2 for all new backend services.

cases.rationale: Python 2 is end-of-life — recommending it for new work is outdated.
